sources_audited.note: The Census NAICS archive directly defines 333995 as establishments manufacturing hydraulic and pneumatic fluid-power cylinders and actuators.
The Census profile reports 298 total employer establishments for 333995 in the 2023 County Business Patterns data.

The BLS table provides the May 2023 occupational mix for three-digit NAICS 333000, including engineering, administrative, business, sales, production, and material-moving employment; the packet discloses the broader-industry bridge.
Pearson documents quotation inputs, diagnostics, machining, resealing, honing, repair, rebuild, remanufacture, new-build options, and pressure testing.
Hydramachine documents stocked cylinders, custom fabrication, precision machining, teardown, inspection, and rebuild services.

The page reports national manufacturing sold-business transaction trends and valuation benchmarks for 2021-2025, supporting that small manufacturers transact, but the inspected page does not report the claimed closed-transaction counts.
The Census 2022 ABS page provides economy-wide employer-business owner-characteristics tables, including owner age; the packet explicitly discloses the economy-wide bridge.
NFPA reports its total shipments index in recovery, an 8.2% forecast 2026 annual growth rate, construction-machinery recovery, and inflation, tariff, price-sensitivity, and margin risks.
NFPA identifies electrification, connectivity, automation, energy efficiency, functional safety, and technology adoption as fluid-power industry trends.
Parker-Hannifin's 2025 Form 10-K documents hydraulic and electromechanical actuators, aftermarket support, and diversified OEM and end-market exposure.

caveats: The a=0.32 task-exposure value is a judgmental mapping from three-digit machinery occupational shares, not a measured six-digit AI-exposure rate; the PROXY label, bridge, and 0.22-0.43 range disclose this limitation.
S4 is a Canadian service provider, so it supports the physical workflow but is only analogous evidence for the stated U.S. lower-middle-market lens.
S6 supports the existence and broad characteristics of sold manufacturing businesses but not the packet's specific claim that the page reports closed-transaction counts.

The s5 estimate combines establishments, broad sold-business evidence, and economy-wide owner-age data; none measures the five-year transfer rate for independent 333995 firms, but this is disclosed as a PROXY with a wide range.
The q sources do not observe productivity-savings retention or contract repricing; the value is an explicitly bridged PROXY and should not be read as measured pass-through behavior.
The d5 bridge extrapolates a one-year, total-fluid-power forecast into a five-year cylinder-and-actuator multiplier; this horizon and product-scope mismatch is disclosed and bounded.
Parker-Hannifin is a global platform-scale supplier excluded from the acquisition lens, so S10 is useful only for broad product and end-market context, not representative target economics.

The lens marks the industry non-heterogeneous even though the packet itself distinguishes repeat OEM production, custom job shops, catalog products, and repair or rebuild models; the primitive ranges and caveats absorb much of this variation, so it does not meet the materiality test.
summary: Mechanics pass and all ten primitive-cited sources were reopened. The record is useful and evidence-state honest: broad occupational, ownership, transaction, pricing, and demand proxies are explicitly bridged with plausible limitations and uncertainty ranges. One source description overstates S6 by referring to closed-transaction counts that are not shown on the inspected page, and several sources are broader than the U.S. LMM lens, but correcting those issues would not change the base LOW_PRIORITY tier or the estimate-driven VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action.
